task_completion.description: The abdominal CT volume is loaded into the application.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 3D Slicer interface is visible, displaying axial, sagittal, and coronal views of the loaded CT scan.

task_completion.description: The axial view is navigated to the correct slice index for the maximum aortic diameter.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The axial slice viewer shows the aorta at its largest cross-section. The slice offset or index indicator in the viewer or Data Probe should indicate a slice index at or very near 45.

task_completion.description: A closed curve markup is accurately placed around the aorta.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A closed curve markup (typically a colored outline) is drawn around the aorta, which is the large, circular blood vessel located immediately anterior to the spine in the axial view.

task_completion.description: The closed curve markup uses a sufficient number of control points.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The closed curve drawn on the aorta has at least 6 distinct control points (dots or nodes) visible along its path.

task_completion.description: The cross-sectional area of the aorta is measured and is within the expected tolerance.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Markups module in 3D Slicer, or the final JSON output, displays a cross-sectional area measurement of approximately 855 mm².

task_completion.description: The measurement results are saved to the specified JSON file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A terminal, file explorer, or text editor shows the creation or contents of 'aortic_area_measurement.json' in the '~/Documents/SlicerData/Exports/' directory, containing fields like patient_id, measurement_type, slice_index, cross_sectional_area_mm2, num_control_points, and curve_name.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the 3D Slicer GUI to perform the measurement.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the 3D Slicer interface with the Markups module open and active interaction with the image viewers, rather than just terminal commands or text editors.

integrity.description: The closed curve was physically drawn on the image.
integrity.visual_evidence: The closed curve is clearly visible overlaying the anatomical structure in the slice viewers, confirming it wasn't just generated programmatically without visual placement.

integrity.description: The JSON file was not manually typed with hardcoded expected values.
integrity.visual_evidence: The values in the JSON file match the actual measurements shown in the Slicer GUI. There should be no evidence of the agent simply typing '855' and '45' into a text editor without deriving them from the Slicer measurement.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The maximum aortic diameter occurs at axial slice index 45. The expected aortic diameter is 33 mm, resulting in a cross-sectional area of approximately 855 mm². The measurement must use at least 6 control points.
